questions.question: Explain why TSA decreases when two solids are joined.
questions.answer: Because the common surfaces between the solids disappear after joining.

questions.question: Why is volume always additive for combined solids?
questions.answer: Because no part of the volume overlaps or disappears.

questions.question: Explain why sphere has minimum surface area for a given volume.
questions.answer: Because its shape distributes volume evenly in all directions.

questions.question: Why is slant height used instead of height in CSA of cone?
questions.answer: Because the curved surface extends along the slant height.

questions.question: Explain why hollow solids have less volume.
questions.answer: Because the hollowed portion removes internal material.

questions.question: Why are hemispherical ends preferred in pressure vessels?
questions.answer: They distribute pressure uniformly and reduce stress.

questions.question: Explain the reason for subtracting base areas in combined solids.
questions.answer: Because those areas are no longer exposed.

questions.question: Why is apparent volume different from actual capacity?
questions.answer: Because raised or hollow portions reduce usable space.

questions.question: Explain why CSA is important in heat loss problems.
questions.answer: Heat transfer depends on exposed surface area.

questions.question: Why is radius kept same while joining solids?
questions.answer: To maintain continuity and smooth surface.

questions.question: Why is cone volume less than cylinder volume?
questions.answer: Because the cone tapers to a point.

questions.question: Explain why curved surfaces are structurally stronger.
questions.answer: They distribute force evenly without stress points.

questions.question: Why is Pythagoras theorem used in cone problems?
questions.answer: To find slant height from radius and height.

questions.question: Why does hollowing affect mass but not outer TSA?
questions.answer: Because outer surface remains unchanged.

questions.question: Explain why sphere is used in ball bearings.
questions.answer: Because it allows smooth motion with minimum friction.

questions.question: Why is TSA used in wrapping problems?
questions.answer: Wrapping covers all exposed surfaces.

questions.question: Why does volume not change during joining?
questions.answer: Because total space occupied remains same.

questions.question: Explain why curved surfaces reduce air resistance.
questions.answer: They allow smooth flow of air.

questions.question: Why is CSA used in paint estimation problems?
questions.answer: Paint covers only exposed curved areas.

questions.question: Why is volume important in storage design?
questions.answer: It determines storage capacity.

questions.question: Explain why curved tanks withstand pressure better.
questions.answer: Pressure is evenly distributed on curved surfaces.

questions.question: Why does TSA change but volume remain same in hollowing?
questions.answer: Because material is removed internally.

questions.question: Explain why sphere encloses maximum volume.
questions.answer: Its shape minimizes surface area for given volume.

questions.question: Why is TSA important in insulation design?
questions.answer: Heat loss depends on exposed area.

questions.question: Why are cylindrical shapes common in containers?
questions.answer: They offer good volume with structural stability.
